factual-accuracy.requirement: States Hasselblad X2D does NOT support Capture One RAW or tethering; Phocus is Hasselblad's native tethering solution
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response explicitly says Capture One Pro does not currently support Hasselblad X2D RAW files and that users rely on Hasselblad’s proprietary Phocus software for tethered shooting.

factual-accuracy.requirement: States Fujifilm GFX100 II X-sync or flash sync speed as 1/125s or 1/125 second
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response states that the Fujifilm GFX100 II flash sync speed “maxes out at ~1/125s,” which satisfies the requirement to state 1/125s (or 1/125 second).

factual-accuracy.requirement: States Hasselblad X2D XCD leaf-shutter lenses sync up to 1/2000s or 1/4000s with specific lens models
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response states that the Hasselblad X2D 100C uses leaf shutter lenses allowing flash sync speeds up to 1/4000s, but it does not mention 1/2000s sync or tie either sync speed to specific XCD lens models.

factual-accuracy.requirement: States Phase One XF IQ4 with Blue Ring/Schneider-Kreuznach leaf-shutter lenses sync up to 1/1600s
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response states that the Phase One XF IQ4 with Schneider Kreuznach leaf shutter lenses can sync “up to 1/1600s depending on lens,” which satisfies the required sync speed claim.

factual-accuracy.requirement: States both Fujifilm GFX100 II and Phase One IQ4 support Capture One for tethering or RAW processing
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response says Fujifilm GFX100 II is supported in Capture One (including a Fujifilm edition) and describes Phase One XF IQ4 150MP as tightly integrated with Capture One Pro/Capture One Inside for tethering and RAW workflow.

factual-accuracy.requirement: States Phase One IQ4 supports Ethernet, Bonjour, or network tethering capability to Capture One
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response states that the Phase One XF IQ4 150MP offers three tether options including Ethernet with Power over Ethernet (PoE), along with USB-C and Wi‑Fi, for use with Capture One Pro.

factual-accuracy.requirement: States Fujifilm GFX100 II body price between $7,000-8,500
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response includes a pricing table that lists the Fujifilm GFX100 II body at $7,499, which falls within the required $7,000–$8,500 range.

factual-accuracy.requirement: States Hasselblad X2D body price between $7,000-8,500
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response includes a pricing table that lists the Hasselblad X2D 100C body-only price as “$7,400 - $8,200,” which falls within the required $7,000–$8,500 range.

factual-accuracy.requirement: States Phase One XF IQ4 complete system between $50,000-70,000 for body, back, and lenses
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response lists the Phase One XF IQ4 150MP as “~$52,000 - $54,000” for a full system, which falls within the $50,000–70,000 range for a complete system (body/back and lenses).

factual-accuracy.requirement: Compares 3-prime lens kit costs (Fuji $6-8k range, Hasselblad $10-12k range, Phase One $15-20k range)
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response discusses lens pricing qualitatively (e.g., Fuji primes $2,000–$4,000; Phase One primes $6,000–$10,000+) but does not provide a 3-prime kit cost comparison in the specified ranges. It never states Fuji $6–8k, Hasselblad $10–12k, and Phase One $15–20k for a 35/80/110-equivalent prime set.

factual-accuracy.requirement: Names at least 2 rental providers for medium-format backup bodies (e.g., FotoCare, Adorama Rental, Lensrentals)
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response names multiple rental providers for backup bodies, including FotoCare and root NYC (and also mentions K&M Camera, CameraRentalsNYC, Scheimpflug, and FutureCapture).

factual-accuracy.requirement: States Hasselblad X2D has 1TB internal SSD storage
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response explicitly states under the Hasselblad X2D 100C workflow section that it has an “Internal 1TB SSD,” indicating 1TB internal SSD storage.

factual-accuracy.requirement: Identifies Hasselblad color science as HNCS or Hasselblad Natural Colour Solution
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response discusses Hasselblad color science as natural and lifelike, but it never identifies it by the specific term “HNCS” or spells out “Hasselblad Natural Colour Solution.”

factual-accuracy.requirement: States or implies Hasselblad X2D works with Capture One for tethering or RAW processing
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response explicitly says Capture One Pro does not currently support Hasselblad X2D RAW files and that users rely on Phocus instead, so it does not claim X2D works with Capture One for tethering or RAW processing.

breadth-and-depth-of-analysis.requirement: Provides conditional recommendations mapping each system to specific user priorities or use case scenarios
breadth-and-depth-of-analysis.explanation: The response gives scenario-based recommendations, explicitly labeling “Best Fit Summary” for each system (value/fast-paced Capture One workflow, flash-heavy leaf-shutter needs, ultra-high fidelity color-critical work) and ties these to the user’s stated priorities like strobe sync, tethering, and workflow speed.

breadth-and-depth-of-analysis.requirement: Identifies Hasselblad requires Phocus (not Capture One) as a strategic workflow ecosystem constraint
breadth-and-depth-of-analysis.explanation: The response explicitly states that Capture One Pro does not support Hasselblad X2D RAW files and that users must rely on Hasselblad’s proprietary Phocus software for tethering and RAW editing, framing this as a workflow constraint.

breadth-and-depth-of-analysis.requirement: Distinguishes Phase One network/Ethernet tethering from USB-only tethering as workflow architecture advantage
breadth-and-depth-of-analysis.explanation: The response explicitly contrasts Phase One’s Ethernet/PoE tethering option against USB-C tethering for Fujifilm and Hasselblad, framing Ethernet/PoE as providing greater tethering flexibility and stability as a workflow architecture advantage.

breadth-and-depth-of-analysis.requirement: Explains HSS mode involves power loss vs leaf-shutter sync maintaining full flash power at high shutter speeds
breadth-and-depth-of-analysis.explanation: The response discusses leaf-shutter sync allowing very high sync speeds (e.g., 1/4000s) versus focal-plane shutter limits, but it never explains that HSS achieves high shutter speeds by pulsing the flash and therefore reduces effective flash power compared with leaf-shutter sync maintaining full flash power.

breadth-and-depth-of-analysis.requirement: Projects total ownership costs over multiple years including depreciation, subscriptions, or recurring fees
breadth-and-depth-of-analysis.explanation: The response includes a “Total Cost of Ownership and System Investment Over 3 Years” section that discusses depreciation rates and recurring software costs (Capture One subscription) over a 3-year horizon, which constitutes multi-year ownership cost projection including depreciation and subscriptions.

breadth-and-depth-of-analysis.requirement: Provides NYC-specific rental coverage or support availability information for the mentioned New York location
breadth-and-depth-of-analysis.explanation: The response includes NYC-specific rental availability by naming local rental houses (e.g., FotoCare, K&M Camera, root NYC, Scheimpflug, CameraRentalsNYC, FutureCapture) and describing backup body rental access and support options in New York.

breadth-and-depth-of-analysis.requirement: Explicitly corrects at least one common misconception about these systems with supporting documentation
breadth-and-depth-of-analysis.explanation: The response explicitly corrects a misconception by stating that Capture One Pro does not currently support Hasselblad X2D RAW files and cites Capture One’s support article as documentation.

breadth-and-depth-of-analysis.requirement: States professional fashion photography doesn't need the specified features or dismisses the user's technical requirements as unnecessary
breadth-and-depth-of-analysis.explanation: The response takes the user’s technical requirements seriously and provides detailed comparisons across sync reliability, tethering/Capture One, color accuracy, workflow speed, lens costs, depreciation, software, and NYC rentals. It does not claim these features are unnecessary or dismiss the requirements as unimportant.

presentation-quality.requirement: Uses domain-specific photography terminology (leaf shutter, focal-plane shutter, tethering, X-sync, HSS, CFexpress, PDAF)
presentation-quality.explanation: The response uses domain-specific photography terminology, explicitly mentioning “leaf shutter” and “focal plane shutter” multiple times, along with related sync concepts like flash sync speed.

presentation-quality.requirement: Organizes information by comparison criteria with headings, bullets, or tables for each evaluation dimension
presentation-quality.explanation: The response is organized by evaluation dimensions using numbered section headings (e.g., strobe sync, tethering, color, workflow, lenses, cost) and provides bullet-point comparisons for each camera, plus summary tables.

presentation-quality.requirement: Maintains professional advisory tone without marketing language, brand bias, or unsupported superlatives
presentation-quality.explanation: The response uses a professional, advisory comparison style and avoids overt marketing language or brand bias. While it includes some superlative phrasing (e.g., “unbeatable,” “perfect,” “highest”), it generally frames claims in a practical, evaluative context rather than hype.

presentation-quality.requirement: Explains fundamental photography concepts like ISO, aperture, or RAW files that a Canon R5 professional would already know
presentation-quality.explanation: The response does not explain basic concepts like ISO, aperture, or what RAW files are. It stays focused on medium-format system comparisons (sync, tethering, color, workflow, costs) without introductory photography fundamentals a Canon R5 professional would already know.

presentation-quality.requirement: Presents camera system comparisons as narrative prose paragraphs instead of structured tables, lists, or side-by-side format
presentation-quality.explanation: The response uses multiple structured elements, including numbered sections with bullet lists and at least two comparison tables (pricing summary and feature comparison), rather than presenting comparisons purely as narrative prose paragraphs.

presentation-quality.requirement: Uses casual language patterns like "honestly", "I think", "seems like a pain", or hedging phrases that undermine professional authority
presentation-quality.explanation: The response maintains a formal, authoritative tone throughout and does not use casual hedges like “honestly,” “I think,” or phrases like “seems like a pain.”

citation-quality.requirement: Cites official manufacturer sites (fujifilm-x.com, hasselblad.com, phaseone.com) for camera specifications
citation-quality.explanation: The response cites official manufacturer sites for specifications, including Fujifilm’s fujifilm-x.com story page, Hasselblad’s hasselblad.com X2D page, and Phase One’s phaseone.com XF system page in the Sources section.

citation-quality.requirement: Cites Capture One support pages (support.captureone.com) for camera compatibility and tethering capabilities
citation-quality.explanation: The response includes a Capture One support.captureone.com link about Hasselblad support and discusses Capture One integration, but it does not cite any Capture One support pages specifically for camera compatibility lists or tethering capability documentation for the compared cameras.

citation-quality.requirement: Cites Schneider-Kreuznach documentation (schneiderkreuznach.com) for Phase One Blue Ring leaf-shutter lens sync speeds
citation-quality.explanation: The response does not cite any Schneider-Kreuznach documentation from schneiderkreuznach.com regarding Phase One Blue Ring leaf-shutter lens sync speeds. It mentions Schneider Kreuznach lenses and gives a sync speed claim, but provides no Schneider-Kreuznach source link or citation. The required schneiderkreuznach.com documentation citation is missing.

citation-quality.requirement: Cites authorized dealers or manufacturer stores (B&H Photo, manufacturer direct stores, Digital Transitions) for pricing
citation-quality.explanation: The response cites authorized dealers/manufacturer stores for pricing, including FotoCare (dealer) and official manufacturer sites for Hasselblad and Phase One. It does not cite B&H Photo or Digital Transitions specifically, but it does include authorized dealer/manufacturer sources.

citation-quality.requirement: Cites established rental providers (FotoCare, Adorama Rental, Lensrentals, or similar) for backup body availability
citation-quality.explanation: The response cites established rental providers for backup availability, explicitly naming FotoCare multiple times and also mentioning other NYC rental houses (e.g., K&M Camera, root NYC, FutureCapture).

citation-quality.requirement: Bases specific claims on "I heard" or secondhand information instead of citing documented sources
citation-quality.explanation: The response does not base claims on “I heard” or other secondhand phrasing. It presents assertions as general statements and includes a sources section with links rather than relying on hearsay.
